questions.question_text: What right does TOPA give tenants in DC when their landlord decides to sell?
questions.correct_answer: A right of first refusal to purchase the property
distractors: A guaranteed lease extension of 2 years
The right to match any buyer's offer after closing
Automatic relocation assistance of $10,000
questions.explanation: TOPA gives DC tenants a right of first refusal when a landlord decides to sell a rental property. Tenants must receive written notice and have a defined period to express interest in purchasing.

questions.question_text: Under TOPA, what is the first step a landlord must take when deciding to sell a rental property?
questions.correct_answer: Provide tenants a written Offer of Sale notice
distractors: List the property on the MLS first
Notify DLCP of their intent to sell
Obtain written tenant approval to list
questions.explanation: The TOPA process begins when the owner provides tenants with a written Offer of Sale notice, informing them of the intent to sell and their rights under the law.

questions.question_text: A DC landlord sells a rental property without providing TOPA notice to tenants. What are the legal consequences?
questions.correct_answer: The sale may be voidable by the tenants
distractors: No consequences if the sale has already closed
A fine of $500 per unit
The tenants must vacate within 90 days
questions.explanation: Failure to comply with TOPA can make the sale voidable. Tenants can challenge the sale and assert their purchase rights. TOPA compliance is a critical step in selling DC rental property.

questions.question_text: If DC tenants decide not to exercise their TOPA rights, what happens next?
questions.correct_answer: The owner may sell to a third party
distractors: The owner must wait 1 year before selling
The tenants automatically get relocation assistance
The owner must reduce the price by 10%
questions.explanation: If tenants waive or fail to exercise their TOPA rights within the required timeframe, the owner may proceed with selling the property to a third-party buyer.

questions.question_text: Which transfers are exempt from TOPA requirements?
questions.correct_answer: Transfers between co-owners or family members
distractors: Sales to investors above market value
Foreclosure sales by any lender
Commercial property sales over $1 million
questions.explanation: TOPA exempts certain transfers including those between co-owners, family members, estate transfers to heirs, and certain court-ordered transfers. Standard market sales are not exempt.

questions.question_text: Under the 2025 RENTAL Act, buildings constructed within the last how many years are exempt from TOPA?
distractors: 5 years
10 years
20 years
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act exempts buildings constructed within the last 15 years from TOPA, though a Notice of Transfer is still required. This encourages new construction in DC.

questions.question_text: A 3-unit building in DC is owned by an individual who owns no other properties. Under the RENTAL Act, is this building exempt from TOPA?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, small 2-4 unit buildings from non-corporate sole owners are exempt
distractors: No, all rental buildings are subject to TOPA regardless of size
Only if every unit is occupied by the owner's family members
Only if the building was originally constructed after 1975
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act exempts 2-4 unit buildings when not owned by a corporate entity or an individual who owns multiple properties in DC. This is the small building exemption.

questions.question_text: When did the RENTAL Act's TOPA changes become effective?
distractors: January 1, 2025
July 1, 2025
March 1, 2026
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act became effective on December 31, 2025. The new construction exemption applies retroactively to the effective date for buildings that received certificates of occupancy before then.

questions.question_text: Under the RENTAL Act, does a newly constructed 8-year-old apartment building need to comply with TOPA when sold?
questions.correct_answer: No, it is exempt as construction within 15 years
distractors: Yes, all apartment buildings must comply
Only if the building has more than 10 units
Only if the tenants are on month-to-month leases
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act exempts buildings constructed within the last 15 years from TOPA requirements. An 8-year-old building falls within this exemption, though a Notice of Transfer is still required.

questions.question_text: The RENTAL Act revised the definition of 'tenant' for TOPA purposes. Under the new definition, who qualifies?
questions.correct_answer: Persons under a written lease who are liable for rent
distractors: Anyone who has ever lived in the building
Any person currently occupying a unit physically
Only tenants with leases of 1 year or more
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act narrowed the tenant definition to persons entitled to occupancy under a written lease (current or expired) who are liable to pay rent, excluding certain occupants without lease obligations.

questions.question_text: Under the RENTAL Act, what is the maximum relocation assistance a tenant can negotiate through TOPA?
questions.correct_answer: The lesser of 1 year's rent or $12,000
distractors: $5,000 regardless of rent amount
3 months' rent with no dollar cap
6 months' rent or $25,000
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act caps negotiable relocation assistance at the lesser of one year's rent or $12,000 (adjusted annually). This is one of the consideration limits imposed on TOPA negotiations.

questions.question_text: In a building with 5 or more units, how long must a tenant organization wait before assigning TOPA purchase rights to a third party?
distractors: 15 days
30 days
90 days
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act imposes a 45-day cooling-off period for buildings with 5+ units before tenant organizations can assign purchase rights to third parties. For buildings under 5 units, the period is 22 days.

questions.question_text: Under the RENTAL Act, which of the following can tenants negotiate for through TOPA?
questions.correct_answer: Building affordability caps on rental rates
distractors: Cash payments to individual tenants exceeding $12,000
Ownership shares in the new owner's LLC
A guaranteed right to purchase in 5 years
questions.explanation: Tenants may negotiate for relocation assistance (capped), building affordability, organizing expenses, and specified improvements. Cash payments beyond the cap and equity interests are not among the permitted considerations.

questions.question_text: What must a TOPA sale notice disclose under the RENTAL Act?
questions.correct_answer: Availability of no-cost tenant support providers
distractors: The seller's net proceeds from the sale
Names of all prospective buyers
The commission rate of the listing agent
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act requires sale notices to disclose the availability of no-cost technical assistance and list certified Tenant Support Providers who can help tenants evaluate their TOPA rights.

questions.question_text: For buildings with fewer than 5 units, the cooling-off period before tenants can assign TOPA rights is:
distractors: 10 days
30 days
45 days
questions.explanation: The RENTAL Act sets a 22-day cooling-off period for buildings with fewer than 5 units, compared to 45 days for larger buildings. During this period, tenants cannot assign purchase rights to third parties.
